name: Sort-of-Waldorf
intro: molasses, olive oil, and a scant ½ teaspoon salt until well combined. Pour this

ingredients.item: cup/50 g shelled cobnuts or hazelnuts
red cabbage, finely shredded (4¼ cups/300 g)
celery stalks, cut into ¼-inch/5-mm slices (3½ cups/350 g)
Granny Smith apples, cored and thinly sliced (2¾ cups/300 g)
medium red onion, thinly sliced (½ cup/60 g)
cup/50 g finely chopped dill rounded ¾ cup/100 g dried sour cherries or cranberries (optional) salt and black pepper
small shallot, finely chopped (2 tbsp/20 g)

method: To make the mayonnaise, place the shallot, egg yolk, mustard, maple syrup, vinegar, and ½ teaspoon salt in the bowl of a small food processor. Whisk together, then, with the machine still running, slowly add the oils in a steady stream until you get a smooth and thick mayonnaise. Set aside.

Place the cabbage, celery, apples, and onion in a large bowl. Add the sour cream, dill, mayonnaise, sour cherries, ½ teaspoon salt, and some black pepper. Use your hands to thoroughly mix everything together—don’t worry if you break the apple slices; it’s all part of the look—t ransfer to individual plates, scatter the nuts on top, and serve.
